decks.notes: The main concept of this Mono Green Aggro deck is to quickly establish a board presence with efficient creatures such as Badgermole Cub and Mossborn Hydra, while ramping up mana with cards like Llanowar Elves and Earthbender Ascension. The deck also includes powerful support cards like Archdruid's Charm and Innkeeper's Talent to further strengthen the board and disrupt the opponent. With a focus on aggression and tempo, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of early aggression and powerful late-game threats.

This deck focuses on quickly playing creatures and spells to deal damage to your opponent as fast as possible. The goal is to overwhelm your opponent with aggressive plays and efficient removal spells. Cards like Burst Lightning and Sear help to control the board while Eddymurk Crab and Slickshot Show-Off apply pressure. The deck also includes card draw and mana fixing to ensure consistent plays. Overall, the strategy is to be proactive and maintain pressure on the opponent to secure a quick victory.

This deck focuses on fast and aggressive play, utilizing creatures with abilities that benefit from being aggressive, such as Bloodghast and Flamewake Phoenix. The key strategy is to quickly deal damage to the opponent by attacking with a mix of creatures and using burn spells. Cards like Bloodthorn Flail and Moonshadow help to pump up creatures for more damage output. The deck also includes a mix of removal and protection spells to maintain board control and protect key creatures. Overall, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a relentless assault.

This deck focuses on utilizing various cards and abilities to bring powerful creatures back from the graveyard and comboing them with other spells to overwhelm opponents. By constantly recycling creatures and spells from the graveyard, the deck aims to create a strong board presence and dominate the game with powerful combos. The Reanimator strategy is supported by a mix of control and card-draw spells to ensure a steady flow of resources and options throughout the match. The combination of Superior Spider-Man and Deceit provides a stable core for the deck, while the other cards offer additional synergies and utility.

The main strategy of this Izzet Control deck is to control the board with a variety of spells and abilities while outlasting the opponent. By generating card advantage with spells like Accumulate Wisdom and Abandon Attachments, the deck can consistently remove threats and draw into more powerful spells. The deck also includes some aggressive options like Combustion Technique and Firebending Lesson to apply pressure when needed. Additionally, the inclusion of Monument to Endurance allows for extra card draw and resilience against removal. Overall, the deck aims to control the pace of the game and outvalue the opponent through careful resource management.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly build up a board presence with low-cost creatures like Elusive Otter and Slickshot Show-Off, while using spells like Burst Lightning and Torch the Tower to remove any threats from the opponent. The deck also focuses on card draw and manipulation with cards like Opt, Sleight of Hand, and Stock Up. By utilizing a combination of aggressive creatures and burn spells, the deck aims to put pressure on the opponent and secure a victory through fast-paced gameplay and efficient card advantage.

The main concept of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures and powerful spells in order to quickly deal damage and win the game. By using cards like Ashling, Rekindled and Flamebraider, along with support spells such as Deceit and Wistfulness, the deck aims to establish board presence and keep up pressure on the opponent. The mana base is set up to support the aggressive game plan, allowing for quick and efficient casting of spells. This deck focuses on tempo and aggression to secure victories in Standard 1 vs 1 matches.

The main idea of this deck is to consistently bring powerful creatures back from the graveyard by combining a variety of cards that enable reanimation strategies. By using cards like Deceit and Requiting Hex to fill the graveyard with key creatures like Superior Spider-Man and Doomsday Excruciator, the deck can quickly bring them back onto the battlefield. Additionally, using cards like Insatiable Avarice and Stock Up to draw more cards and find the needed combo pieces. The deck focuses on setting up a powerful board state and overwhelming the opponent with strong creatures.

The key concept of this Azorius Aggro deck is to maintain board control and pressure the opponent with a mix of aggressive creatures and powerful spells. By utilizing cards like Aang, Swift Savior and Floodpits Drowner, the deck aims to quickly establish a dominant board presence while disrupting the opponent's strategy with cards like Get Lost and Spell Snare. The inclusion of Voice of Victory and Avatar's Wrath provides additional ways to close out the game efficiently. Overall, the deck focuses on quickly overwhelming the opponent while maintaining control and tempo throughout the match.

The primary focus of this deck is to quickly establish board presence through aggressive creature cards like Elusive Otter and Slickshot Show-Off. The deck also relies on burn spells like Burst Lightning and versatile card draw options like Opt and Sleight of Hand to maintain card advantage and deal direct damage to the opponent. The mana base is centered around blue and red sources to support the aggressive tempo of the deck, with cards like Stormchaser's Talent and Secret Identity offering additional utility and flexibility in different game situations.

The deck focuses on controlling the opponent's board through counterspells and removal spells, while also utilizing card draw and scrying effects to maintain card advantage. Eddymurk Crab and Hearth Elemental provide early game defense, while Sunderflock and Burst Lightning offer removal for threats. Opt and Sleight of Hand help dig for key cards, while Winternight Stories can manipulate the opponent's deck. The inclusion of a mix of counterspells and removal spells allows for versatile answers to opponent's threats. The deck's mana base allows for consistent casting of spells and abilities.

The deck focuses on controlling the game using counter spells, card draw, and removal spells to disrupt the opponent's strategy. The creatures in the deck also provide additional control elements, with Eddymurk Crab and Sunderflock being able to tap down or bounce opponent's creatures. The aim is to slow down the opponent's game plan, while building up resources and eventually taking over the game with powerful spells and creatures. The deck is designed to outlast and outmaneuver the opponent until it can secure a victory.

This mono green aggro deck focuses on quickly building a board presence with efficient creatures like Badgermole Cub and Sazh's Chocobo. It aims to overwhelm the opponent with early aggression and pump spells like Mightform Harmonizer and Earthbender Ascension. The deck also includes some control elements like Royal Treatment and Archdruid's Charm to deal with threats. The mana base is optimized for consistency with cards like Llanowar Elves and Fabled Passage. The goal is to win the game quickly through efficient creature beats and combat tricks.

This deck focuses on playing aggressively with a mix of creatures and spells that deal damage directly to the opponent. Ashling, Rekindled is a key card that can gradually increase in power as the game progresses. Deceit and Flamebraider provide additional damage sources, while cards like Vibrance and Wistfulness help to maintain tempo. Roaming Throne and Sunderflock provide additional threats on the board. The deck also includes removal spells like Bounce Off and Ill-Timed Explosion to control the board. The mana base is carefully constructed to ensure the deck can cast its spells efficiently.

This Simic Aggro deck focuses on fast and efficient creatures to put pressure on the opponent early in the game. The deck aims to maintain a strong board presence and overwhelm the opponent with a mix of aggressive creatures and disruptive spells. With a variety of creatures and spells that provide utility, card advantage, and evasion, the deck can adapt to different matchups and playstyles. By utilizing powerful cards like Craterhoof Behemoth and Oko, Lorwyn Liege, the deck can quickly close out games and secure victories before the opponent can stabilize.

The deck focuses on controlling the game through a combination of counterspells, removal spells, card draw, and board wipes. It utilizes powerful control cards like Day of Judgment and Ultima to clear the board, while also having access to strong finishers like Elspeth, Storm Slayer and Ajani, Outland Chaperone. The deck also includes cards like Rest in Peace and Aang's Iceberg to disrupt opponent strategies and maintain control of the game. With a mix of removal, counter magic, and card advantage, this UW Control deck aims to outlast opponents and secure victory through careful manipulation of the game state.
